title: Deep Recon Operation
learning_objectives: Execute comprehensive OSINT sweeps using automated tools
Mine Certificate Transparency logs for subdomain discovery
Identify and assess cloud asset exposure
Correlate multi-source intelligence into attack surface maps
scenario: A fintech company in Johannesburg has engaged your team for a comprehensive external assessment. The CTO is confident their attack surface is minimal. Your job: prove them wrong using advanced OSINT techniques.

scenario_context.setting: External pentest of fintech startup in Johannesburg with defensive CTO
scenario_context.challenge: Discover comprehensive external attack surface despite organizational confidence in security posture
scenario_context.relevance: Applies to any organization underestimating external exposure, common in African tech sector

subtasks.title: Automated OSINT Sweep
subtasks.description: Use theHarvester and Recon-ng to enumerate subdomains, emails, IP ranges.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Deploy theHarvester against target domain using multiple sources (Google, Bing, LinkedIn). Configure Recon-ng with API keys for enhanced coverage. Document all unique findings across tools.

subtasks.title: Certificate Transparency Mining
subtasks.description: Query crt.sh and CT log sources. Map subdomains to IPs.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use crt.sh API to retrieve all certs for target domain and wildcards. Cross-reference with other CT logs. Build subdomain-to-IP mapping with temporal analysis.
subtasks.evidence_required: CT log results + subdomain-to-IP mapping document

subtasks.title: Code Repository Search
subtasks.description: Search GitHub, GitLab, Pastebin for leaked credentials or config files.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Conduct targeted searches using employee names, domain name variations, common terms. Evaluate sensitivity of each finding. Document all discovered assets.
subtasks.evidence_required: Repository search results with sensitive findings documented

subtasks.title: Cloud Asset Discovery
subtasks.description: Probe for exposed S3 buckets, Azure blobs, misconfigured CDNs.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Test common bucket naming patterns. Use cloud enumeration tools. Document access levels and data exposure. Assess compliance implications.
subtasks.evidence_required: Cloud asset enumeration results with access assessment

subtasks.title: Correlated Intelligence Report
subtasks.description: Cross-reference all findings into unified attack surface map.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Consolidate findings from all sources. Identify relationships between assets. Prioritize by risk. Create visual attack surface diagram.
subtasks.evidence_required: Attack surface report with risk ratings and visual map

reflection_prompt: How did your OSINT discoveries challenge the organization's assumptions about their security posture? What patterns emerge when correlating data from multiple sources?

quiz.question: Which Certificate Transparency log source is most reliable for comprehensive subdomain discovery?
quiz.explanation: crt.sh aggregates multiple CT logs, providing comprehensive coverage. While other sources exist, crt.sh is the standard aggregation point.

quiz.question: What is the primary risk of exposed S3 buckets in cloud asset discovery?
quiz.explanation: Exposed S3 buckets can lead to unauthorized access and data exfiltration. This is a critical exposure that must be identified and reported.

mentor_notes: Assess not just tool usage but the intelligence analyst's ability to think critically about data quality and correlation. Check for completeness of source coverage and reasonable assessment of false positives.
